title: Bridge Essay: Mental imagery: The role of primary visual cortex in aphantasia.
concept: Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**Mental imagery: The role of primary visual cortex in aphantasia.** — Curr Biol (2024). DOI: 10.1016/j.cub.2024.09.076. PMID: 39500282. The neural underpinnings of aphantasia - a phenomenon where people have an absence of mental imagery - are poorly understood. Two recent studies provide the first direct evidence linking brain activation in primary visual cortex to the imagery deficits in aphantasia.

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 10 (Imaginal Reconstruction). Evaluation: Excellent (score=5.1)
